audit_items.proof_trace: All internal pointer dereferences in addentry.c are properly guarded: `tmp->lm_chain` is accessed only when `tmp != NULL` (loop condition), `tmp->lm_chain->lm_chain_tail` is guarded by `if (tmp->lm_chain)`, `(*list)->lm_chain_tail` in the else branch is safe because `prev != NULL` implies the original list head was non-NULL, and `(*list)->lm_chain_tail` in ldap_add_result_entry is guarded by `if (*list)`. Parameter NULL-checks rely on `assert()` which is standard C library precondition enforcement for public API functions; these functions have no internal callers in the codebase, so null-pointer safety of parameters is a caller contract by design.
